hook: CBC World (reporting Trump) and Iran opposition media — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Strait of Hormuz / US-Iran deal. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 34 buckets, 61 articles, 5 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: SECURITY_DEFENSE (5 buckets). "Iran Must Only Succeed Once to Trigger a Catastrophe" — German military expert
Frame: EXTERNAL_REGULATION (5 buckets). "Trump calls ceasefire counter-proposal from Iran 'totally unacceptable'" — CBC World
Frame: ECONOMIC (4 buckets). "The closure of the Strait has led to a sudden spike in the price of oil and gas from the Middle East. This has had particularly serious imp…" — The Diplomat

Frame: POLICY_PRESCRIPTION (3 buckets). "called for the restoration of the safe, unimpeded, and continuous transit passage of vessels and aircraft in the Strait of Hormuz" — The Diplomat / ASEAN Summit

The paradox: Both sides simultaneously claim their own proposals are reasonable while categorically rejecting the other's, revealing a framing deadlock where convergence appears impossible. (CBC World (reporting Trump) & Iran opposition media, opposite blocs.)

Silence as data: `brazil` didn't cover this — they covered Saudi Arabia's military response and regional pressure (Rubio's diplomacy, MbS's airspace denial) — tactical statecraft rather than proposal content. instead.
**The Diplomat** (asia_pacific_regional): ASEAN frames Strait reopening as prerequisite for collective energy security and food price stability; mobilizes regional institutions as unified actor.
